text:
The web search has returned multiple authoritative sources confirming the exact paper exists. The Springer article (result index 3 and 6) shows the author name as "Brüschweiler, R." in the formal citation, while the full name "Rafael Brüschweiler" appears in the text. The cited reference shows "Rafael Br ̈uschweiler" which appears to be a formatting/encoding issue with the umlaut character (ü rendered as "̈u" in the citation), but this is a minor formatting variation, not an author name error.

The paper metadata across multiple sources confirms:
- 
Title: "PPM: a side-chain and backbone chemical shift predictor for the assessment of protein conformational ensembles" by Li, DW., Brüschweiler, R., published in J Biomol NMR 54, 257–265 (2012)

- 
Da-Wei Li and Rafael Brüschweiler authored this paper, published in J. Biomol. NMR 54, 257-265, (2012)


The automated checker identified only a minor author name formatting difference (abbreviation "R." vs. full name "Rafael" and the umlaut encoding), but the paper is definitely real and matches the cited reference.

EXPLANATION: The paper exists with the exact title and correct authors Da-Wei Li and Rafael Brüschweiler published in J. Biomol NMR in 2012. The umlaut encoding issue in "Br ̈uschweiler" is a minor formatting artifact, not an author name error.
LINK: https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s10858-012-9668-8
FOUND_TITLE: PPM: a side-chain and backbone chemical shift predictor for the assessment of protein conformational ensembles
FOUND_AUTHORS: Da-Wei Li, Rafael Brüschweiler
FOUND_YEAR: 2012
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
